name: Jcvision and Associates, INC
description_short: Non-profit housing and tax assistance organization providing free mortgage modification help, tax representation, and fair housing services to low-income Georgia residents.
description_long:
JCVision and Associates, Inc. is a Georgia-based non-profit organization that has been operating since at least 2003, with a tax assistance program established that year. The organization serves low to moderate-income households throughout middle and southeast Georgia, with a physical office in Hinesville, GA. Their mission centers on financial stability and housing security, reflected in their organizational motto: 'Without a Financial Vision the people perish.'

The organization offers multiple core services without charge: free mortgage modification application processing and submission to lenders for homeowners facing foreclosure or payment arrears; low-income tax clinic (LITC) representation for federal tax disputes with the IRS, including Tax Court representation and negotiation services; pre-purchase and post-purchase home buyer education and counseling; fair housing rights education and tenant-landlord dispute resolution specific to Georgia law; and a Healthy Homes Program focused on affordable housing development in rural communities like Riceboro. They actively recruit volunteer attorneys, accountants, and enrolled agents for their pro bono tax panel.

JCVision distinguishes itself through genuinely free services—they explicitly state they complete mortgage modification paperwork and lender submission at no cost, unusual for housing counseling. Their tax program offers direct representation in Tax Court, not just advisory services. They operate fair housing testing programs as community accountability tools. Their Healthy Homes Program targets climate-resilient affordable housing in underserved rural areas rather than urban markets.

As a non-profit, JCVision's reach is geographically limited to Georgia (primarily middle and southeast regions) and their services depend on volunteer professional resources. Their website provides limited detail on program eligibility criteria, success rates, or processing timelines. While their free-service model is genuine, capacity constraints typical of non-profits may affect availability.
pros: Completely free mortgage modification application processing and lender submission—no hidden fees
Direct representation in Tax Court and IRS disputes through LITC program, not just basic counseling
Operating tax assistance program since 2003 demonstrates 20+ years of stability and experience
Certified housing counselors available for foreclosure prevention counseling
Fair housing testing program provides community enforcement mechanism against discrimination
Explicitly targets underserved populations: low-income, moderate-income, and rural communities
Accepts cryptocurrency donations, indicating willingness to serve unbanked/underbanked populations
cons: Geographic limitation to Georgia (primarily middle and southeast) excludes most U.S. consumers
Website provides no information on eligibility criteria, application processing timelines, or program capacity
Non-profit dependent on volunteer professionals—tax and legal services availability may be unpredictable
Limited detail on mortgage modification approval rates or success metrics provided
No online application portal visible; requires phone contact (912) 877-4243 or in-person office visit for services
best_for: Low-income Georgia homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage payment arrears seeking free modification help
Low to moderate-income Georgia taxpayers in IRS disputes who cannot afford professional tax representation
Georgia renters or housing consumers experiencing fair housing violations or tenant-landlord disputes
Rural Georgia communities seeking affordable housing development assistance and healthy homes programs
diagnosis: JCVision is best for Georgia residents (primarily middle and southeast regions) with low to moderate income who face mortgage arrears, IRS tax disputes, or housing rights violations and need completely free professional representation. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Georgia and non-profit capacity constraints—services depend on volunteer professional availability and may have wait times or limited slots.
services: Free mortgage modification application preparation and lender submission
Foreclosure prevention counseling from certified housing counselors
Low-Income Tax Clinic (LITC) representation in IRS disputes and Tax Court
Federal tax return audit representation and IRS negotiation services
Pre-purchase home buyer education and counseling
Post-purchase homeowner education and financial stability counseling
Fair housing rights education and dispute resolution
Tenant-landlord law consultation specific to Georgia
Healthy Homes Program—affordable, safe, climate-resilient housing development
Community financial stability assessment and planning
Pro bono volunteer recruitment for tax and legal services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 135 E Ml King Jr Dr, Suite G 104, Hinesville, GA 31313
